audiobookshelf.0.1.12:
image:
  repository: ghcr.io/advplyr/audiobookshelf
  tag: 2.19.0
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

replicaCount: 1

service:
  port: 80

persistence:
  config:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: ""
    size: 10Gi
    claimName: audiobookshelf-config
    mountPath: /config
  metadata:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: ""
    size: 20Gi
    claimName: audiobookshelf-metadata
    mountPath: /metadata
  audiobooks:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: ""
    size: 100Gi
    claimName: audiobookshelf-books
    mountPath: /audiobooks
  podcasts:
    enabled: false
    storageClassName: ""
    size: 10Gi
    claimName: audiobookshelf-podcasts
    mountPath: /podcasts

env: {}
# Example overrides:
# env:
#   HOST: 127.0.0.1
#   PORT: "13378"
#   TZ: "UTC"
#   FFMPEG_PATH: ffmpeg
#   FFPROBE_PATH: ffprobe

istio-ingress:
  enabled: false
  virtualService:
    route:
      host: audiobookshelf
      port: 80

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

ingress:
  enabled: false
  hosts:
    - host: audiobookshelf.local
      paths:
        - /

backups:
  enabled: false
  image: mbround18/backup-cron:v1.0.0
  schedule: 0 3 * * *
  retainNDays: "7"
  retainNFiles: "10"

resources: {}

serviceAccount:
  create: true
  name: ""

secrets:
  # JWT secret config
  jwt:
    create: false
    name: audiobookshelf-jwt
    key: JWT_SECRET_KEY

nodeSelector: {}
tolerations: []
affinity: {}

# Pod security and chown init container
securityContext:
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  fsGroup: 1000

initChown:
  # If enabled, an initContainer will chown the mounted volumes to the rootless UID/GID
  enabled: true
  image: busybox:1.37.0
  # Extra directories the chown init container should ensure (optional)
  extraDirs: []

enshrouded.0.3.13:
# Default values for enshrouded.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

# The number of replicas to create
replicaCount: 1

# Environment variables to pass to the application
environment: []
  # - name: MY_ENV_VAR
#   value: my-env-var-value

# The name of the storage class to use. If not set, the default storage class will be used.
storageClassName: ""

# Configuration for backups
backups:
  # Whether backups are enabled
  enabled: false
  # The image to use for backups
  image: mbround18/backup-cron:v1.0.0
  # The schedule for backups, in cron format
  schedule: "*/5 * * * *"
  # The number of days to retain backups
  retentionDays: 7
  # The number of backup files to keep. If 0, all backup files are kept.
  filesToKeep: 0
  # The configuration for the backup PVC
  pvc:
    name: backups
    size: 10Gi

# Configuration for the application image
image:
  repository: mbround18/enshrouded-docker
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: v2.0.1

# The secrets to use for pulling images
imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

# Configuration for the service account
serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

# Annotations to add to the pod
podAnnotations: {}
# Labels to add to the pod
podLabels: {}

# The security context for the pod
podSecurityContext:
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

# The security context for the container
securityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop: [ALL]

# Configuration for the application endpoint
endpoint:
  externalIP: ""
  gamePort: 15636
  queryPort: 15637

# The resources to allocate for the pod
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 2Gi
  limits:
    memory: 4Gi

# Configuration for autoscaling
autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

# The configuration for the deployment PVC
deployment:
  pvc:
    name: game-data
    size: 20Gi

# Additional volumes on the output Deployment definition.
volumes:
  - name: game-data
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: enshrouded-game-data
  - name: backups
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: enshrouded-backups

# Additional volumeMounts on the output Deployment definition.
volumeMounts:
  - name: game-data
    mountPath: /home/steam/enshrouded
    readOnly: false
  - name: backups
    mountPath: /home/steam/backups
    readOnly: true

# The node selector to use for the pod
nodeSelector: {}

# The tolerations to use for the pod
tolerations: []

# The affinity to use for the pod
affinity: {}

enshrouded.0.3.15:
# Default values for enshrouded.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

# The number of replicas to create
replicaCount: 1

# Environment variables to pass to the application
environment: []
  # - name: MY_ENV_VAR
#   value: my-env-var-value

# The name of the storage class to use. If not set, the default storage class will be used.
storageClassName: ""

# The configuration for the backup PVC (only provisioned when backup-job.enabled is true)
backups:
  pvc:
    name: backups
    size: 10Gi

# Configuration for the application image
image:
  repository: mbround18/enshrouded-docker
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: v2.0.1

# The secrets to use for pulling images
imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

# Configuration for the service account
serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

# Annotations to add to the pod
podAnnotations: {}
# Labels to add to the pod
podLabels: {}

# The security context for the pod
podSecurityContext: &podSecurityContext
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

# The security context for the container
securityContext: &securityContext
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop: [ALL]

# Configuration for the application endpoint
endpoint:
  externalIP: ""
  gamePort: 15636
  queryPort: 15637

# The resources to allocate for the pod
resources: &resources
  requests:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 2Gi
  limits:
    memory: 4Gi

# Configuration for autoscaling
autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

# The configuration for the deployment PVC
deployment:
  pvc:
    name: game-data
    size: 20Gi

# Additional volumes on the output Deployment definition.
volumes:
  - name: game-data
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: enshrouded-game-data

# Additional volumeMounts on the output Deployment definition.
volumeMounts:
  - name: game-data
    mountPath: /home/steam/enshrouded
    readOnly: false

# Settings for the reusable backup-job subchart (charts/backup-job).
# Set enabled: true to provision the backups PVC and run scheduled backups.
backup-job:
  enabled: false
  schedule: "*/5 * * * *"
  podSecurityContext: *podSecurityContext
  securityContext: *securityContext
  resources: *resources
  env:
    - name: INPUT_FOLDER
      value: "/home/steam/enshrouded/savegame"
    - name: OUTPUT_FOLDER
      value: "/home/steam/backups/"
    - name: OUTPUT_USER
      value: "1000"
    - name: OUTPUT_GROUP
      value: "1000"
    - name: RETAIN_N_DAYS
      value: "7"
    - name: RETAIN_N_FILES
      value: "0"
  volumes:
    - name: game-data
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: enshrouded-game-data
    - name: backups
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: enshrouded-backups
  volumeMounts:
    - name: game-data
      mountPath: /home/steam/enshrouded
      readOnly: true
    - name: backups
      mountPath: /home/steam/backups

# The node selector to use for the pod
nodeSelector: {}

# The tolerations to use for the pod
tolerations: []

# The affinity to use for the pod
affinity: {}

      mountPath: /home/steam/backups

# The node selector to use for the pod
nodeSelector: {}

# The tolerations to use for the pod
tolerations: []

# The affinity to use for the pod
affinity: {}
enshrouded.0.3.17:
# Default values for enshrouded.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

# The number of replicas to create
replicaCount: 1

# Environment variables to pass to the application
environment: []
  # - name: MY_ENV_VAR
#   value: my-env-var-value

# The name of the storage class to use. If not set, the default storage class will be used.
storageClassName: ""

# The configuration for the backup PVC (only provisioned when backup-job.enabled is true)
backups:
  pvc:
    name: backups
    size: 10Gi

# Configuration for the application image
image:
  repository: mbround18/enshrouded-docker
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: v2.0.1

# The secrets to use for pulling images
imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

# Configuration for the service account
serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

# Annotations to add to the pod
podAnnotations: {}
# Labels to add to the pod
podLabels: {}

# The security context for the pod
podSecurityContext: &podSecurityContext
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

# The security context for the container
securityContext: &securityContext
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop: [ALL]

# Configuration for the application endpoint
endpoint:
  externalIP: ""
  gamePort: 15636
  queryPort: 15637

# The resources to allocate for the pod
resources: &resources
  requests:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 2Gi
  limits:
    memory: 4Gi

# Configuration for autoscaling
autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

# The configuration for the deployment PVC
deployment:
  pvc:
    name: game-data
    size: 20Gi

# Additional volumes on the output Deployment definition.
volumes:
  - name: game-data
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: enshrouded-game-data

# Additional volumeMounts on the output Deployment definition.
volumeMounts:
  - name: game-data
    mountPath: /home/steam/enshrouded
    readOnly: false

# Settings for the reusable backup-job subchart (charts/backup-job).
# Set enabled: true to provision the backups PVC and run scheduled backups.
backup-job:
  enabled: false
  schedule: "*/5 * * * *"
  podSecurityContext: *podSecurityContext
  securityContext: *securityContext
  resources: *resources
  env:
    - name: INPUT_FOLDER
      value: "/home/steam/enshrouded/savegame"
    - name: OUTPUT_FOLDER
      value: "/home/steam/backups/"
    - name: OUTPUT_USER
      value: "1000"
    - name: OUTPUT_GROUP
      value: "1000"
    - name: RETAIN_N_DAYS
      value: "7"
    - name: RETAIN_N_FILES
      value: "0"
  volumes:
    - name: game-data
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: enshrouded-game-data
    - name: backups
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: enshrouded-backups
  volumeMounts:
    - name: game-data
      mountPath: /home/steam/enshrouded
      readOnly: true
    - name: backups
      mountPath: /home/steam/backups

# The node selector to use for the pod
nodeSelector: {}

# The tolerations to use for the pod
tolerations: []

# The affinity to use for the pod
affinity: {}

forgejo.0.1.5:
replicaCount: 1

nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

image:
  repository: codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo
  tag: "15"
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

imagePullSecrets: []

serviceAccount:
  create: true
  automount: false
  annotations: {}
  name: ""

podAnnotations: {}

podSecurityContext:
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch
  seccompProfile:
    type: RuntimeDefault

securityContext:
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  runAsNonRoot: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  http:
    port: 3000
  ssh:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 22
    nodePort: null

ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: ""
  annotations: {}
  hosts:
    - host: forgejo.local
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: Prefix
  tls: []

resources: {}

autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 3
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: null

nodeSelector: {}
affinity: {}
tolerations: []

persistence:
  enabled: true
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  size: 10Gi
  storageClass: ""

postgresql:
  enabled: true

externalSecrets:
  enabled: true
  # Specifies the name of the ClusterSecretStore or SecretStore to use.
  secretStore: vault-kv
  # The name of the Kubernetes Secret resource that will be created by ExternalSecrets.
  secretName: forgejo-external-secrets
  # Maps the keys in the generated Kubernetes Secret to the remote secret provider's keys.
  keys:
    # Key for the database password.
    databasePassword: "forgejo/data/databasePassword" # Example path in Vault
    # Key for the runner registration token.
    runnerToken: "forgejo/data/runnerToken" # Example path in Vault
    # Key for the GitHub token used by the mirror cron job.
    githubToken: "forgejo/data/githubToken" # Example path in Vault
    # Key for the Forgejo token used by the mirror cron job.
    forgejoToken: "forgejo/data/forgejoToken" # Example path in Vault

config:
  database:
    db_type: postgres
    host: postgresql
    name: forgejo
    user: forgejo
  server:
    domain: forgejo.local
    ssh_domain: forgejo.local
    root_url: http://forgejo.local/
    http_port: "3000"
  mirror:
    default_interval: 8h
    min_interval: 10m
  actions:
    enabled: "true"
  security:
    install_lock: "true"
  service:
    disable_registration: "true"
    enable_notify_mail: "false"

runner:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: code.forgejo.org/forgejo/runner
    tag: "12.9.0"
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  labels: docker
  dockerSocket:
    mount: true
    path: /var/run/docker.sock
    # WARNING: Mounting the Docker socket (docker.sock) directly into a container
    # can pose a significant security risk. It effectively gives the container
    # root access to the host's Docker daemon, allowing it to create, manage,
    # and even escape containers, potentially compromising the host system.
    # Consider alternatives like Docker-in-Docker (dind) or other secure build
    # mechanisms if possible. Only enable this if you understand and accept
    # the security implications.

mirrorCronJob:
  enabled: false
  schedule: "0 * * * *"
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/patnei/github2forgejo
    tag: latest
  github_user_or_org: ""
  forgejo_user: ""
  github_token: ""
  forgejo_token: ""

foundryvtt.0.2.20:
replicaCount: 1
fullnameOverride: foundryvtt-dnd-boop-ninja

########################################################
# Main Foundry container settings
########################################################
image:
  repository: mbround18/foundryvtt-docker
  tag: v3.0.27
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

sslProxy: false

network:
  host: dnd.boop.ninja
  tls:
    enabled: true
    credentialName: foundry-tls

ingress:
  enabled: false
  annotations: {}
  className: ""
  hosts:
    - host: foundry.local
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: ImplementationSpecific

istio-ingress:
  enabled: false
  virtualService:
    route:
      host: foundry
      port: 80

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  annotations: {}
  port: 80

imagePullSecrets: []
priorityClassName: ""
terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "0.3"
    memory: 1024Mi
  requests:
    cpu: "0.2"
    memory: 512Mi

########################################################
# Pod security and service account
########################################################
serviceAccount:
  create: true
  name: ""
  annotations: {}

podSecurityContext:
  enabled: true
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch
  seccompProfile:
    type: RuntimeDefault

securityContext:
  enabled: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  runAsNonRoot: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
  capabilities:
    drop: [ALL]

podAnnotations: {}
commonLabels: {}

nodeSelector: {}
affinity: {}
tolerations: []

env: []

initImage:
  repository: busybox
  tag: 1.37.0
cleanupImage:
  repository: alpine
  tag: "20260127"

updateStrategy: {}

pdb:
  enabled: false
  minAvailable: 1
  # maxUnavailable: 0

########################################################
# Probes
########################################################
probes:
  liveness:
    enabled: true
    path: /
    initialDelaySeconds: 30
    periodSeconds: 10
  readiness:
    enabled: true
    path: /
    initialDelaySeconds: 10
    periodSeconds: 10

########################################################
# Key Foundry volumes
# (Each results in a volumeClaimTemplate if enabled)
########################################################
volumeClaimTemplates:
  backups:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: longhorn
    size: 10Gi
    accessModes: [ReadWriteMany]
  config:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: longhorn
    size: 1Gi
    accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  data:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: longhorn
    size: 20Gi
    accessModes: [ReadWriteMany]
  core:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: longhorn
    size: 2Gi
    accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]

fvtt-dndbeyond-companion.0.0.13:
# Default values for fvtt-dndbeyond-companion.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

replicaCount: 1

image:
  repository: mbround18/fvtt-dndbeyond-companion
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: v1.1.0

imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

podAnnotations: {}

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 8745

ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: ""
  annotations: {}
    # kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
    # kubernetes.io/tls-acme: "true"
  hosts:
    - host: chart-example.local
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: ImplementationSpecific
  tls: []
  #  - secretName: chart-example-tls
  #    hosts:
  #      - chart-example.local

resources: {}
  # We usually recommend not to specify default resources and to leave this as a conscious
  # choice for the user. This also increases chances charts run on environments with little
  # resources, such as Minikube. If you do want to specify resources, uncomment the following
  # lines, adjust them as necessary, and remove the curly braces after 'resources:'.
  # limits:
  #   cpu: 100m
  #   memory: 128Mi
  # requests:
  #   cpu: 100m
  #   memory: 128Mi

autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

nodeSelector: {}

tolerations: []

affinity: {}

grafana.0.1.0:
# Global settings for the entire application stack
global:
  domain: "example.com"
  environment: "production"
  # Global Istio settings
  istio:
    namespace: "istio-system"

# Main Grafana application configuration
grafana:
  # Controls anonymous access to Grafana
  anonymous:
    enabled: false
  # Admin user password management. Create a secret with a 'password' key.
  # Example: kubectl create secret generic grafana-admin-creds --from-literal=password='your-secret-password'
  admin:
    existingSecret: "grafana-admin-creds"
  # Persistence configuration using a PVC
  persistence:
    enabled: true
    size: "10Gi"
    storageClassName: "standard"
  # External database configuration (PostgreSQL)
  database:
    type: "postgres"
    host: "postgres-postgresql.database.svc.cluster.local"
    port: 5432
    name: "grafana"
    user: "grafana"
    existingSecret: "grafana-db-credentials" # Secret with 'password' key

# Istio Ingress configuration
istio-ingress:
  enabled: true
  virtualService:
    hosts:
      - "grafana.example.com"
  tls:
    enabled: true
    mode: "SIMPLE" # Assumes TLS termination at the gateway
    credentialName: "grafana-tls-cert" # Secret containing tls.crt and tls.key

# GitOps Tools (ArgoCD) configuration
gitops-tools:
  argoCd:
    mode: "auto" # 'enabled', 'disabled', or 'auto'
    instanceLabel: "primary"
    commonAnnotations:
      notifications.argoproj.io/subscribe.on-deployed: "grafana-deploys"

# Upstream Grafana chart values
# We alias it to 'upstream-grafana' to avoid conflicts and make it clear
# which values are being passed to the subchart.
upstream-grafana:
  # Pass admin password from our secret
  admin:
    existingSecret: "grafana-admin-creds"
    userKey: "user" # Assuming user is also in the secret, otherwise remove
    passwordKey: "password"

  # Enable provisioning of dashboards and datasources from ConfigMaps
  sidecar:
    dashboards:
      enabled: true
      label: "grafana_dashboard"
    datasources:
      enabled: true
      label: "grafana_datasource"

  # Configure persistence
  persistence:
    enabled: true
    type: "pvc"
    size: "10Gi"
    storageClassName: "standard"
    accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce

  # Use an external database
  grafana.ini:
    database:
      type: "postgres"
      host: "postgres-postgresql.database.svc.cluster.local"
      port: 5432
      name: "grafana"
      user: "grafana"
      # Password will be mounted from the secret
    auth.anonymous:
      enabled: false

  # ServiceMonitor settings for Prometheus
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: true
    labels:
      release: "prometheus" # Label to match your Prometheus Operator

  # Mount the database password secret
  extraSecretMounts:
    - name: grafana-db-secret-mount
      secretName: "grafana-db-credentials"
      mountPath: "/etc/grafana/secrets"
      readOnly: true

hytale.0.1.21:
# Default values for hytale.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

replicaCount: 1

# Environment variables to pass to the application
environment:
  - name: PATCHLINE
    value: release
  - name: SERVER_PORT
    value: "5520"
  - name: SERVER_BIND
    value: 0.0.0.0
  - name: DISABLE_SENTRY
    value: "false"
  - name: BOOT_COMMAND
    value: auth login device
  - name: REMOTE_CONSOLE_PORT
    value: "7000"

# Optional: derive JAVA_OPTS from container resources (requests/limits)
# When enabled, remove JAVA_OPTS from the environment list to avoid duplicates.
javaOpts:
  auto: true
  name: JAVA_OPTS
  # Env vars populated from resources.*.memory using the Downward API
  limitEnv: JAVA_MEM_LIMIT
  requestEnv: JAVA_MEM_REQUEST
  # Divisor for resourceFieldRef; "1Mi" yields integer Mi values
  divisor: 1Mi
  # Suffix appended to the generated -Xmx/-Xms values
  unitSuffix: m

# The name of the storage class to use. If not set, the default storage class will be used.
storageClassName: ""

# Configuration for the application image
image:
  repository: mbround18/hytale
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: "v0.0.11"

imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

podAnnotations: {}
podLabels: {}

podSecurityContext:
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

securityContext:
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000

endpoint:
  externalIP: ""
  serverPort: 5520
  # consolePort: 7000

resources:
  # We usually recommend not to specify default resources and to leave this as a conscious
  # choice for the user. This also increases chances charts run on environments with little
  # resources, such as Minikube. If you do want to specify resources, uncomment the following
  # lines, adjust them as necessary, and remove the curly braces after 'resources:'.
  limits:
    memory: 8Gi
  requests:
    memory: 1Gi

# Health check probes for the game server
probes:
  liveness: # Still WIP
    enabled: false
    # Check if the UDP game port is listening
    initialDelaySeconds: 60
    periodSeconds: 30
    timeoutSeconds: 5
    failureThreshold: 3
    successThreshold: 1
  readiness: # Still WIP
    enabled: false
    # Check if the server is ready to accept connections
    # High initial delay and failure threshold to account for game/mod downloads on first boot
    initialDelaySeconds: 300
    periodSeconds: 30
    timeoutSeconds: 5
    failureThreshold: 20
    successThreshold: 1

pvc:
  name: data
  size: 10Gi
  # Path where the persistent volume will be mounted in the container
  # Defaults to /data/server so /data/.staging and other temp files can be wiped on restart
  mountPath: /data/server

service:
  # Whether to create the externally-accessible Service (NodePort/ClusterIP).
  # Set to false to omit creating the service (useful when using Ingress or to avoid NodePort resources).
  create: true
  # Type of the external Service. Common values: NodePort, ClusterIP, LoadBalancer
  type: NodePort
  # Explicit nodePort for the server (only has effect when type=NodePort). Use null to let k8s auto-assign
  nodePortServer: 32520
  # Explicit nodePort for the console (only has effect when type=NodePort)
  nodePortConsole:
  # Service annotations (useful for MetalLB or other controllers)
  annotations: {}

initChown:
  # When enabled, an init container will chown mounted volumes to the non-root UID/GID
  enabled: true
  # A small image with sh and chown (default: busybox). Can be overridden if your distro needs different tools.
  image: busybox:1.37.0
  # Extra directories to chown in addition to /data
  extraDirs: []

# Optional envFrom references to import many env vars from a ConfigMap or Secret
envFrom:
  # Name of a ConfigMap whose keys should be imported as env vars (optional)
  configMapName: ""
  # If true, the configMapRef will be marked optional
  configMapOptional: false

  - name: DISABLE_SENTRY
    value: "false"
  - name: BOOT_COMMAND

  - name: REMOTE_CONSOLE_PORT
    value: "7000"

# When enabled, remove JAVA_OPTS from the environment list to avoid duplicates.
javaOpts:
  auto: true
  name: JAVA_OPTS

  limitEnv: JAVA_MEM_LIMIT
  requestEnv: JAVA_MEM_REQUEST
  # Divisor for resourceFieldRef; "1Mi" yields integer Mi values
  divisor: 1Mi
  # Suffix appended to the generated -Xmx/-Xms values
  unitSuffix: m

# The name of the storage class to use. If not set, the default storage class will be used.
storageClassName: ""

# Configuration for the application image
image:
  repository: mbround18/hytale
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: "v0.0.11"

imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""

  commonLabels: {}

  automount: true

  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.

  name: ""

podLabels: {}

  capabilities:

  runAsNonRoot: true

istio-ingress.0.1.9:
# Istio Ingress Subchart Configuration

# Enable/disable the entire subchart
enabled: true

# Gateway configuration
gateway:
  enabled: true
  # Generate gateway name from app label if not specified
  name: ""
  namespace: ""

  # Pod selector for the Istio ingress gateway
  selector:
    istio: ingress

  # Gateway servers - can be overridden completely or merged
  servers:
    - port:
        number: 80
        protocol: HTTP
        name: http
      hosts:
        - "*"

# VirtualService configuration
virtualService:
  enabled: true
  # Generate name from parent chart if not specified
  name: ""
  namespace: ""

  # Hosts for the VirtualService
  hosts:
    - ""

  # Gateway references (auto-filled if using local gateway)
  gateways: []

  # HTTP routes - can be extended with custom routes
  http: []
    # Example:
    # - name: "api-route"
    #   match:
    #     - uri:
    #         prefix: "/api"
    #   route:
    #     - destination:
    #         host: api.default.svc.cluster.local
    #         port:
    #           number: 8080

  # TLS routes (for passthrough/SNI routing)
  tls: []

  # TCP routes
  tcp: []

  # Export to other namespaces
  exportTo: []

# TLS configuration
tls:
  enabled: false
  mode: SIMPLE # SIMPLE, MUTUAL, AUTO_PASSTHROUGH, ISTIO_MUTUAL

  # For Kubernetes: secret name with tls.crt and tls.key
  credentialName: ""

  # For file paths (non-Kubernetes)
  serverCertificate: ""
  privateKey: ""

  # HTTPS redirect
  httpsRedirect: false

  # Optional: CA certificate for MUTUAL mode
  caCertificates: ""
  caCertCredentialName: ""

  # TLS settings
  minProtocolVersion: TLSV1_2
  maxProtocolVersion: ""
  cipherSuites: []

# DNS annotations (external-dns integration)
externalDns:
  enabled: false
  # Annotations to add for DNS
  annotations:
    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: ""
    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/ttl: "3600"

# Helpers
helpers:
  # If true, will automatically construct gateway reference from local gateway
  useLocalGateway: true

  # If true, will append VirtualService routes instead of replacing
  mergeRoutes: false

# Override mode: "virtualservice" (default) or "gateway"
resourceType: "virtualservice"

# Allow complete override of Gateway spec
gatewayOverride: {}

# Allow complete override of VirtualService spec
virtualServiceOverride: {}

meilisearch.0.1.21:
global:
  secret:
    annotations: {}

image:
  repository: getmeili/meilisearch
  tag: v1.42.1
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

# Optional Meilisearch UI sidecar
ui:
  enabled: false
  image:
    repository: riccoxie/meilisearch-ui
    tag: v0.14.1
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Base path for the UI (e.g., /manage)
  basePath: /manage
  # Service port for the UI
  port: 24900
  # Enable ingress for UI
  ingress:
    enabled: false
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      memory: 128Mi

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi
  limits:
    memory: 2Gi

persistence:
  enabled: true
  accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
  size: 10Gi

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 7700
  # UI service port (only if ui.enabled)
  uiPort: 24900

ingress:
  enabled: false
  # Standard Kubernetes Ingress (fallback if istio not enabled)
  className: ""
  # Default domain if not specified
  domain: meilisearch.example.com
  # Custom hosts (overrides default domain)
  hosts: []
  # Annotations
  annotations: {}
  # TLS configuration
  tls:
    enabled: false
    secretName: ""

# Istio Ingress (istio-ingress subchart)
istio-ingress:
  enabled: false
  virtualService:
    enabled: true
    hosts:
      - meilisearch.example.com
    # http routes are dynamically generated based on ui.enabled
    # see templates/00-merge-istio-routes.yaml
  gateway:
    enabled: true
    selector:
      istio: ingress
    servers:
      - port:
          number: 80
          protocol: HTTP
          name: http
        hosts:
          - "*"
  # TLS configuration (optional - enable for HTTPS)
  tls:
    enabled: false
    mode: SIMPLE
    credentialName: ""
    httpsRedirect: false

secrets:
  masterKey:
    name: meilisearch-master-key
    key: MEILI_MASTER_KEY
    create: true

provisioning:
  enabled: false
  # The secret where the generated API key will be stored
  indexSecret: meilisearch-api-key
  # Key description in Meilisearch
  apiKeyDescription: Provisioned API Key
  # Actions allowed for this key
  apiKeyActions: ["*"]
  # Indexes allowed for this key
  apiKeyIndexes: ["*"]

openobserve.0.1.0:
# Default values for openobserve.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

image:
  repository: public.ecr.aws/zinclabs/openobserve
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart's appVersion.
  tag: "latest"

imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created.
  create: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account.
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template.
  name: ""

podAnnotations:
  prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
  prometheus.io/port: "5080"
  prometheus.io/path: "/metrics"

podSecurityContext: {}
  # fsGroup: 2000

securityContext: {}
  # capabilities:
  #   drop:
  #   - ALL
  # readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  # runAsNonRoot: true
  # runAsUser: 1000

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  annotations: {}
  ports:
    http: 5080
    grpc: 5081

# Configuration for OpenObserve
config:
  # ZO_LOCAL_MODE=false to enable S3 and Postgres
  localMode: false
  # RUST_LOG level
  logLevel: info
  # Enable/disable telemetry
  telemetry: true
  # ZO_MEMORY_CACHE_MAX_SIZE as a percentage of memory limit
  memoryCacheMaxSizePercentage: 50
  # S3 configuration (non-sensitive parts)
  s3:
    bucket: "openobserve"
    serverUrl: "s3.amazonaws.com"
    region: "us-east-1"

ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: "istio"
  annotations: {}
    # kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
    # kubernetes.io/tls-acme: "true"
  hosts:
    - host: chart-example.local
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: ImplementationSpecific
  tls: []
  #  - secretName: chart-example-tls
  #    hosts:
  #      - chart-example.local

istio:
  enabled: true
  gatewayAnnotations: {}
  virtualServiceAnnotations: {}
  hosts:
    - o2.example.com
  # For GRPC, we need to specify it here
  grpcPaths:
    - /v1/otlp/*
  tls: []
    # - secretName: openobserve-tls
    #   hosts:
    #     - o2.example.com

persistence:
  enabled: true
  storageClass: "longhorn"
  size: 20Gi
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce

resources:
  # We usually recommend not to specify default resources and to leave this as a conscious
  # choice for the user. This also increases chances charts run on environments with little
  # resources, such as Minikube. If you do want to specify resources, uncomment the following
  # lines, adjust them as necessary, and remove the curly braces after 'resources:'.
  limits:
    cpu: 2
    memory: 4Gi
  requests:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 1Gi

externalSecrets:
  enabled: true
  secretStore: my-secret-store
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the secret that will be created
  secretName: openobserve-secret
  # Keys to be fetched from the secret store
  keys:
    rootEmail: "ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL"
    rootPassword: "ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD"
    s3AccessKey: "ZO_S3_ACCESS_KEY"
    s3SecretKey: "ZO_S3_SECRET_KEY"
    databaseUrl: "ZO_DATABASE_URL"

serviceMonitor:
  enabled: false
  namespace: ""
  interval: 30s
  scrapeTimeout: 10s
  labels: {}
  annotations: {}

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 10
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

nodeSelector: {}

tolerations: []

affinity: {}

opentelemetry-collector.0.1.0:
#
# PRODUCTION-GRADE OTEL COLLECTOR VALUES
#

nameOverride: "otel-collector"
fullnameOverride: "otel-collector"

# -- Deployment strategy, can be 'deployment' or 'daemonset'
# For gateway a 'deployment' is preferred
# For agent-per-node a 'daemonset' is preferred
kind: "deployment"

replicaCount: 1

image:
  repository: otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Default tag is .Chart.AppVersion
  tag: "0.150.1-386"

imagePullSecrets: []

# -- Annotations for the Collector Pods
podAnnotations: {}

# -- Security Context for the Pod
podSecurityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 10001
  runAsGroup: 10001
  fsGroup: 10001

# -- Security Context for the Collector container
securityContext:
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL

# -- Kubernetes resource requests and limits
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 1Gi
  requests:
    cpu: 200m
    memory: 256Mi

# -- The core configuration for the OpenTelemetry Collector
# This section directly translates to the otel-config.yaml
# See: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/collector/configuration/
config:
  #
  # RECEIVERS
  #
  receivers:
    otlp:
      protocols:
        grpc:
          endpoint: "0.0.0.0:4317"
        http:
          endpoint: "0.0.0.0:4318"

  #
  # PROCESSORS
  #
  # NOTE: The 'memory_limiter' and 'batch' processors are automatically added with sane defaults.
  processors:
    # Example:
    # k8sattributes:
    #   auth_type: "serviceAccount"
    #   passthrough: false
    #   filter:
    #     node_from_env_var: KUBE_NODE_NAME
    #   extract:
    #     metadata:
    #       - "k8s.pod.name"
    #       - "k8s.pod.uid"
    #       - "k8s.deployment.name"
    #       - "k8s.namespace.name"
    #       - "k8s.node.name"
    #       - "k8s.pod.start_time"
    resourcedetection:
      detectors: ["env", "gcp", "ec2", "ecs", "eks"]
      timeout: 2s
      override: false

  #
  # EXPORTERS
  #
  exporters:
    # Example:
    # otlp/tempo:
    #   endpoint: "tempo.mimir.svc.cluster.local:4317"
    #   tls:
    #     insecure: true
    logging:
      loglevel: debug

  #
  # EXTENSIONS
  #
  extensions:
    health_check: {}
    memory_ballast:
      # In general, this should be set to 1/3 of the memory allocated to the collector.
      # It is not used by default but can be enabled.
      size_mib: 0

  #
  # SERVICE (PIPELINES)
  #
  service:
    extensions: [health_check]
    pipelines:
      # -- Traces pipeline
      traces:
        receivers: [otlp]
        processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
        exporters: [logging]
      # -- Metrics pipeline
      metrics:
        receivers: [otlp]
        processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
        exporters: [logging]
      # -- Logs pipeline
      logs:
        receivers: [otlp]
        processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
        exporters: [logging]

# -- Dynamic Memory Limiter
# Automatically calculates the memory_limiter's `limit_mib` based on a percentage
# of the Kubernetes memory limit, ensuring they never drift apart.
memory_limiter:
  # -- Enable the dynamic memory limiter processor
  enabled: true
  # -- The percentage of `resources.limits.memory` to use for the memory limiter
  percentage_of_limit: 75
  # -- The spike limit as a percentage of the limit
  spike_percentage: 25

# -- Batch Processor Tuning
# Provides sane defaults for high-throughput scenarios to minimize syscalls.
batch_processor:
  # -- Enable the batch processor
  enabled: true
  # -- Timeout for sending a batch
  timeout: 1s
  # -- Number of events to buffer before sending a batch
  send_batch_size: 8192

# -- Hot-reloading of config via a ConfigMap checksum annotation on the deployment
config_reload:
  enabled: true

# -- RBAC settings for processors like `k8sattributes` and `resourcedetection`
rbac:
  # -- Specifies whether RBAC resources should be created
  create: true
  serviceAccount:
    # -- Specifies whether a service account should be created
    create: true
    # -- The name of the service account to use.
    # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
    name: ""
    annotations: {}

# -- NetworkPolicy to lock down ingress traffic
networkPolicy:
  # -- Keep disabled by default so collector endpoints are open within the cluster.
  enabled: false
  # -- Allows traffic from the Prometheus operator namespace for scraping metrics
  allowPrometheusScraping: false
  # -- Namespace names allowed to scrape when allowPrometheusScraping=true.
  prometheusNamespaces:
    - monitoring

# -- Service definitions
service:
  main:
    enabled: true
    type: ClusterIP
    ports:
      grpc:
        port: 4317
        targetPort: 4317
        protocol: TCP
      http:
        port: 4318
        targetPort: 4318
        protocol: TCP

  # -- A headless service for client-side load balancing (useful for gateway deployments)
  headless:
    enabled: false
    ports:
      grpc:
        port: 4317
        protocol: TCP
      http:
        port: 4318
        protocol: TCP

# -- Self-Observability Settings
observability:
  # -- Enable scraping the collector's own metrics
  enabled: true
  # -- Create a ServiceMonitor for the Prometheus Operator
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: true
    namespace: "" # Defaults to .Release.Namespace
    interval: 15s
    scrapeTimeout: 10s
    labels: {}
    annotations: {}
  # -- Alternatively, use pod annotations for scraping
  podAnnotations:
    prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
    prometheus.io/port: "8888" # Default telemetry port
    prometheus.io/path: "/metrics"

  enabled: true
  # -- Timeout for sending a batch
  timeout: 1s
  # -- Number of events to buffer before sending a batch
  send_batch_size: 8192

# -- Hot-reloading of config via a ConfigMap checksum annotation on the deployment
config_reload:
  enabled: true

# -- RBAC settings for processors like `k8sattributes` and `resourcedetection`
rbac:
  # -- Specifies whether RBAC resources should be created
  create: true
  serviceAccount:
    # -- Specifies whether a service account should be created
    create: true
    # -- The name of the service account to use.
    # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
    name: ""
    annotations: {}

# -- NetworkPolicy to lock down ingress traffic
networkPolicy:
  # -- Keep disabled by default so collector endpoints are open within the cluster.
  enabled: false
  # -- Allows traffic from the Prometheus operator namespace for scraping metrics
  allowPrometheusScraping: false
  # -- Namespace names allowed to scrape when allowPrometheusScraping=true.
  prometheusNamespaces:
    - monitoring

# -- Service definitions
service:
  main:
    enabled: true
    type: ClusterIP
    ports:
      grpc:
        port: 4317
        targetPort: 4317
        protocol: TCP
      http:
        port: 4318
        targetPort: 4318
        protocol: TCP

  # -- A headless service for client-side load balancing (useful for gateway deployments)
  headless:
    enabled: false
    ports:
      grpc:
        port: 4317
        protocol: TCP
      http:
        port: 4318
        protocol: TCP

# -- Self-Observability Settings
observability:
  # -- Enable scraping the collector's own metrics
  enabled: true
  # -- Create a ServiceMonitor for the Prometheus Operator
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: true
    namespace: "" # Defaults to .Release.Namespace
    interval: 15s
    scrapeTimeout: 10s
    labels: {}
    annotations: {}
  # -- Alternatively, use pod annotations for scraping
  podAnnotations:
    prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
    prometheus.io/port: "8888" # Default telemetry port
    prometheus.io/path: "/metrics"
opentelemetry-collector.0.1.7:
#
# PRODUCTION-GRADE OTEL COLLECTOR VALUES
#

nameOverride: "otel-collector"
fullnameOverride: "otel-collector"

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

# -- Deployment strategy, can be 'deployment' or 'daemonset'
# For gateway a 'deployment' is preferred
# For agent-per-node a 'daemonset' is preferred
kind: "deployment"

replicaCount: 1

image:
  repository: otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Default tag is .Chart.AppVersion
  tag: "0.153.0-386"

imagePullSecrets: []

# -- Annotations for the Collector Pods
podAnnotations: {}

# -- Security Context for the Pod
podSecurityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 10001
  runAsGroup: 10001
  fsGroup: 10001

# -- Security Context for the Collector container
securityContext:
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL

# -- Kubernetes resource requests and limits
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 1Gi
  requests:
    cpu: 200m
    memory: 256Mi

# -- The core configuration for the OpenTelemetry Collector
# This section directly translates to the otel-config.yaml
# See: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/collector/configuration/
config:
  #
  # RECEIVERS
  #
  receivers:
    otlp:
      protocols:
        grpc:
          endpoint: "0.0.0.0:4317"
        http:
          endpoint: "0.0.0.0:4318"

  #
  # PROCESSORS
  #
  # NOTE: The 'memory_limiter' and 'batch' processors are automatically added with sane defaults.
  processors:
    # Example:
    # k8sattributes:
    #   auth_type: "serviceAccount"
    #   passthrough: false
    #   filter:
    #     node_from_env_var: KUBE_NODE_NAME
    #   extract:
    #     metadata:
    #       - "k8s.pod.name"
    #       - "k8s.pod.uid"
    #       - "k8s.deployment.name"
    #       - "k8s.namespace.name"
    #       - "k8s.node.name"
    #       - "k8s.pod.start_time"
    resourcedetection:
      detectors: ["env", "gcp", "ec2", "ecs", "eks"]
      timeout: 2s
      override: false

  #
  # EXPORTERS
  #
  exporters:
    # Example:
    # otlp/tempo:
    #   endpoint: "tempo.mimir.svc.cluster.local:4317"
    #   tls:
    #     insecure: true
    debug:
      verbosity: detailed

  #
  # EXTENSIONS
  #
  extensions:
    health_check:
      endpoint: "0.0.0.0:13133"

  #
  # SERVICE (PIPELINES)
  #
  service:
    extensions: [health_check]
    pipelines:
      # -- Traces pipeline
      traces:
        receivers: [otlp]
        processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
        exporters: [debug]
      # -- Metrics pipeline
      metrics:
        receivers: [otlp]
        processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
        exporters: [debug]
      # -- Logs pipeline
      logs:
        receivers: [otlp]
        processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
        exporters: [debug]
    telemetry:
      metrics:
        readers:
          - pull:
              exporter:
                prometheus:
                  host: "0.0.0.0"
                  port: 8888

# -- Dynamic Memory Limiter
# Automatically calculates the memory_limiter's `limit_mib` based on a percentage
# of the Kubernetes memory limit, ensuring they never drift apart.
memory_limiter:
  # -- Enable the dynamic memory limiter processor
  enabled: true
  # -- The percentage of `resources.limits.memory` to use for the memory limiter
  percentage_of_limit: 75
  # -- The spike limit as a percentage of the limit
  spike_percentage: 25

# -- Batch Processor Tuning
# Provides sane defaults for high-throughput scenarios to minimize syscalls.
batch_processor:
  # -- Enable the batch processor
  enabled: true
  # -- Timeout for sending a batch
  timeout: 1s
  # -- Number of events to buffer before sending a batch
  send_batch_size: 8192

# -- Hot-reloading of config via a ConfigMap checksum annotation on the deployment
config_reload:
  enabled: true

# -- RBAC settings for processors like `k8sattributes` and `resourcedetection`
rbac:
  # -- Specifies whether RBAC resources should be created
  create: true
  serviceAccount:
    # -- Specifies whether a service account should be created
    create: true
    # -- The name of the service account to use.
    # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
    name: ""
    annotations: {}

# -- NetworkPolicy to lock down ingress traffic
networkPolicy:
  # -- Keep disabled by default so collector endpoints are open within the cluster.
  enabled: false
  # -- Allows traffic from the Prometheus operator namespace for scraping metrics
  allowPrometheusScraping: false
  # -- Namespace names allowed to scrape when allowPrometheusScraping=true.
  prometheusNamespaces:
    - monitoring

# -- Service definitions
service:
  main:
    enabled: true
    type: ClusterIP
    ports:
      grpc:
        port: 4317
        targetPort: 4317
        protocol: TCP
      http:
        port: 4318
        targetPort: 4318
        protocol: TCP

  # -- A headless service for client-side load balancing (useful for gateway deployments)
  headless:
    enabled: false
    ports:
      grpc:
        port: 4317
        protocol: TCP
      http:
        port: 4318
        protocol: TCP

# -- Self-Observability Settings
observability:
  # -- Enable scraping the collector's own metrics
  enabled: true
  # -- Create a ServiceMonitor for the Prometheus Operator
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: true
    namespace: "" # Defaults to .Release.Namespace
    interval: 15s
    scrapeTimeout: 10s
    labels: {}
    annotations: {}
  # -- Alternatively, use pod annotations for scraping
  podAnnotations:
    prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
    prometheus.io/port: "8888" # Default telemetry port
    prometheus.io/path: "/metrics"

palworld.0.3.13:
# Default values for palworld.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

replicaCount: 1

environment: []
  # - name: MY_ENV_VAR
  #   value: my-env-var-value

storageClassName: ""

backups:
  enabled: false
  image: mbround18/backup-cron:v1.0.0
  schedule: "*/5 * * * *"
  retainNDays: 7
  retainNFiles: 0

image:
  repository: mbround18/palworld-docker
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: "2.1"

imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

podAnnotations: {}
podLabels: {}

podSecurityContext:
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

securityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop: [ALL]

endpoint:
  externalIP: ""
  gamePort: 8211
  queryPort: 27015

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 2Gi
  limits:
    memory: 6Gi

autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

pvcs:
  backups:
    name: palworld-backups
    size: 10Gi
    path: /home/steam/backups
  saved:
    name: palworld-saves
    size: 10Gi
    path: /home/steam/palworld/Pal/Saved
    subPath: Saved
  server:
    name: palworld-data
    size: 30Gi
    path: /home/steam/palworld

# Additional volumes on the output Deployment definition.
volumes: []

# Additional volumeMounts on the output Deployment definition.
volumeMounts: []
# - name: foo
#   mountPath: "/etc/foo"
#   readOnly: true

nodeSelector: {}

tolerations: []

affinity: {}

  name: ""

  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

securityContext: &securityContext
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop: [ALL]

endpoint:
  externalIP: ""
  gamePort: 8211
  queryPort: 27015
  # Pinned so a Service replace/recreate doesn't reassign different external ports.
  gameNodePort: 32285
  queryNodePort: 30764

# Run as an Agones GameServer instead of a StatefulSet + NodePort Service.
# When enabled, the StatefulSet, headless Service, and NodePort Service are
# not rendered — Agones owns the pod lifecycle and external port allocation
# instead. PVCs are still created and mounted the same way in both modes.
#
# Caveat: the mbround18/palworld-docker image has no Agones SDK integration,
# so nothing inside the container ever calls the SDK's Ready()/Health() RPCs.
# The GameServer will sit in "Scheduling"/"RequestReady" and never reach
# "Ready" for allocation unless you supply a sidecar/wrapper that watches the
# game port and then calls the local SDK (localhost:{sdkServer.httpPort}) to
# mark it ready. `health.disabled: true` avoids Agones killing the pod for
# never reporting health in the meantime.
agones:
  enabled: false
  # Dynamic: Agones picks a free host port. Static: use the values below as-is.
  # Passthrough: container reads the assigned port from env vars.
  portPolicy: Dynamic
  scheduling: Packed
  health:
    disabled: true
    periodSeconds: 15
    failureThreshold: 3
    initialDelaySeconds: 90
  sdkServer:
    logLevel: Info
    grpcPort: 9357
    httpPort: 9358

resources: &resources
  requests:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 2Gi
  limits:
    memory: 6Gi

autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

pvcs:
  backups:
    name: palworld-backups
    size: 10Gi
    path: /home/steam/backups
  saved:
    name: palworld-saves
    size: 10Gi
    path: /home/steam/palworld/Pal/Saved
    subPath: Saved
  server:
    name: palworld-data
    size: 30Gi
    path: /home/steam/palworld

# Settings for the reusable backup-job subchart (charts/backup-job).
# Set enabled: true to provision the backups PVC and run scheduled backups.
backup-job:
  enabled: false
  schedule: "*/5 * * * *"
  podSecurityContext: *podSecurityContext
  securityContext: *securityContext
  resources: *resources
  env:
    - name: INPUT_FOLDER
      value: "/home/steam/palworld/Pal/Saved/"
    - name: OUTPUT_FOLDER
      value: "/home/steam/backups/"
    - name: OUTPUT_USER
      value: "1000"
    - name: OUTPUT_GROUP
      value: "1000"
    - name: RETAIN_N_DAYS
      value: "7"
    - name: RETAIN_N_FILES
      value: "0"
  volumes:
    - name: palworld-saves
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: palworld-saves
    - name: palworld-backups
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: palworld-backups
  volumeMounts:
    - name: palworld-saves
      mountPath: /home/steam/palworld/Pal/Saved
      subPath: Saved
    - name: palworld-backups
      mountPath: /home/steam/backups

pvc-watcher.0.3.3:
namespace:
  # The name of the Kubernetes namespace where resources will be deployed
  name: pvc-scaler
  # Set to true to create the namespace if it doesn't already exist
  create: true

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

serviceAccount:
  # The name of the ServiceAccount to be used by the Deployment
  name: pvc-scaler

clusterRole:
  # The name of the ClusterRole to be assigned to the ServiceAccount
  name: pvc-scaler-role

clusterRoleBinding:
  # The name of the ClusterRoleBinding that binds the ClusterRole to the ServiceAccount
  name: pvc-scaler-rolebinding

deployment:
  # The name of the Kubernetes Deployment
  name: pvc-scaler
  # The number of replicas (pods) to be created for the Deployment
  replicas: 1
  # The strategy used to replace old pods with new ones (e.g., Recreate or RollingUpdate)
  strategy: Recreate
  # The label used to identify resources related to this Deployment
  appLabel: pvc-scaler
  container:
    # The name of the container within the pod
    name: pvc-scaler
    # Container image details, split into name and tag for flexibility
    image:
      name: mbround18/k8s-pvc-watcher
      tag: ""
    # The policy for pulling the container image (Always, IfNotPresent, Never)
    imagePullPolicy: Always
    resources:
      # Resource requests define the minimum amount of resources required by the container
      requests:
        memory: 128Mi # Minimum amount of memory requested for the container
        cpu: 500m # Minimum amount of CPU requested for the container
      # Resource limits define the maximum amount of resources the container can use
      limits:
        memory: 256Mi # Maximum amount of memory the container is allowed to use
        cpu: 1000m # Maximum amount of CPU the container is allowed to use

syncthing.0.1.11:
image:
  repository: syncthing/syncthing
  tag: 2.0.16
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

replicaCount: 1

service:
  port: 8384

persistence:
  data:
    enabled: true
    storageClassName: ""
    size: 30Gi
    claimName: syncthing-data
    mountPath: /var/syncthing

env: {}

istio-ingress:
  enabled: false
  virtualService:
    route:
      host: syncthing
      port: 8384

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

ingress:
  enabled: false
  hosts:
    - host: syncthing.local
      paths:
        - /

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 100m
    memory: 128Mi
  limits:
    memory: 512Mi

serviceAccount:
  create: true
  name: ""

secrets:
  # JWT secret config
  password:
    create: false
    name: syncthing-password
    key: PASSWORD

nodeSelector: {}
tolerations: []
affinity: {}

# Pod security and chown init container
securityContext:
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

initChown:
  # If enabled, an initContainer will chown the mounted volumes to the rootless UID/GID
  enabled: true
  image: syncthing/syncthing:2.0.16
  # Extra directories the chown init container should ensure (optional)
  extraDirs: []

valheim.0.4.13:
# Default values for valheim.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

# The number of replicas to create
replicaCount: 1

# Gamesettings, PUID should no be changed
PUID: &containerpid "111"
GUID: &containergid "1000"
#
GAMEPORT: &gameport "2456"
HTTPPORT: &httpport "8080"

# Environment variables to pass to the application, see https://github.com/mbround18/valheim-docker for possible values
environment:
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_DAYS_TO_LIVE
    value: "7"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_ON_SHUTDOWN
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_ON_UPDATE
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_REMOVE_OLD
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_SCHEDULE
    value: "*/15 * * * *"
  - name: AUTO_UPDATE
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_UPDATE_PAUSE_WITH_PLAYERS
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_UPDATE_SCHEDULE
    value: 12 * * * *
  - name: ENABLE_CROSSPLAY
    value: "0"
  - name: GUID
    value: *containergid
  - name: HTTP_PORT
    value: *httpport
  - name: NAME
    value: Valheim Docker
  - name: PASSWORD
    value: Strong! Password @ Here
  - name: PORT
    value: *gameport
  - name: PUBLIC
    value: "1"
  - name: PUID
    value: *containerpid
  - name: TZ
    value: America/Chicago
  - name: UPDATE_ON_STARTUP
    value: "1"
  - name: WORLD
    value: Dedicated

# The name of the storage class to use. If not set, the default storage class will be used.
storageClassName: ""

#NodePort or LoadBalancer
service:
  type: LoadBalancer
  #this is ignored if type is not NodePort (Range: 30000-32767 - make sure your starting port is at least 3 ports below 32767)
  nodePortGame: 31232
  # if you use MetalLB, add the correct annotations here to select i.e. the pool and or the ip
  # hint: the annotations changed in recent versions of metallb from metallb.universe.tf to metallb.io
  # if the ipaddresspool in use has `autoassign: true` you can specify the ip you want to assign, without autoassign you also need to specify the name
  # leaving out the annotations and assuming autoassign: true you get the next free ip from an available pool
  annotations:
    metallb.io/loadBalancerIPs: 10.100.14.175
  #  metallb.io/address-pool: mypool

# Configuration for the application image
image:
  registry: docker.io
  repository: mbround18/valheim
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag, default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: "3.6"

# The secrets to use for pulling images
imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

# Configuration for the service account
serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: false
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

# Annotations to add to the pod
podAnnotations: {}
# Labels to add to the pod
podLabels: {}

# The security context for the pod
podSecurityContext:
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

# The security context for the container
# Note: valheim-docker uses s6-overlay (starts as root, drops to PUID).
# runAsNonRoot cannot be set here without breaking the init process.
securityContext:
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  seccompProfile:
    type: RuntimeDefault

# The resources to allocate for the pod
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 1Gi
  limits:
    memory: 4Gi

# Configuration for autoscaling
autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

# The configuration for the deployment PVC
deployment:
  pvc:
    name: data
    size: 10Gi

saves:
  pvc:
    name: saves
    size: 10Gi

backup:
  pvc:
    name: backups
    size: 10Gi

# The node selector to use for the pod
nodeSelector: {}

# The tolerations to use for the pod
tolerations: []

# The affinity to use for the pod
affinity: {}

  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

# The configuration for the deployment PVC
deployment:
  pvc:
    name: data
    size: 10Gi

saves:
  pvc:
    name: saves
    size: 10Gi

backup:
  pvc:
    name: backups
    size: 10Gi

# The node selector to use for the pod
nodeSelector: {}

# The tolerations to use for the pod
tolerations: []

# The affinity to use for the pod
affinity: {}
valheim.0.4.15:
# Default values for valheim.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

# The number of replicas to create
replicaCount: 1

# Gamesettings, PUID should no be changed
PUID: &containerpid "111"
GUID: &containergid "1000"
#
GAMEPORT: &gameport "2456"
HTTPPORT: &httpport "8080"

# Environment variables to pass to the application, see https://github.com/mbround18/valheim-docker for possible values
environment:
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_DAYS_TO_LIVE
    value: "7"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_ON_SHUTDOWN
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_ON_UPDATE
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_REMOVE_OLD
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_BACKUP_SCHEDULE
    value: "*/15 * * * *"
  - name: AUTO_UPDATE
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_UPDATE_PAUSE_WITH_PLAYERS
    value: "1"
  - name: AUTO_UPDATE_SCHEDULE
    value: 12 * * * *
  - name: ENABLE_CROSSPLAY
    value: "0"
  - name: GUID
    value: *containergid
  - name: HTTP_PORT
    value: *httpport
  - name: NAME
    value: Valheim Docker
  - name: PASSWORD
    value: Strong! Password @ Here
  - name: PORT
    value: *gameport
  - name: PUBLIC
    value: "1"
  - name: PUID
    value: *containerpid
  - name: TZ
    value: America/Chicago
  - name: UPDATE_ON_STARTUP
    value: "1"
  - name: WORLD
    value: Dedicated

# The name of the storage class to use. If not set, the default storage class will be used.
storageClassName: ""

#NodePort or LoadBalancer
service:
  type: LoadBalancer
  #this is ignored if type is not NodePort (Range: 30000-32767 - make sure your starting port is at least 3 ports below 32767)
  nodePortGame: 31232
  # if you use MetalLB, add the correct annotations here to select i.e. the pool and or the ip
  # hint: the annotations changed in recent versions of metallb from metallb.universe.tf to metallb.io
  # if the ipaddresspool in use has `autoassign: true` you can specify the ip you want to assign, without autoassign you also need to specify the name
  # leaving out the annotations and assuming autoassign: true you get the next free ip from an available pool
  annotations:
    metallb.io/loadBalancerIPs: 10.100.14.175
  #  metallb.io/address-pool: mypool

# Configuration for the application image
image:
  registry: docker.io
  repository: mbround18/valheim
  pullPolicy: Always
  # Overrides the image tag, default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: "3.6"

# The secrets to use for pulling images
imagePullSecrets: []
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

# Configuration for the service account
serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: false
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

# Annotations to add to the pod
podAnnotations: {}
# Labels to add to the pod
podLabels: {}

# The security context for the pod
podSecurityContext:
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch

# The security context for the container
# Note: valheim-docker uses s6-overlay (starts as root, drops to PUID).
# runAsNonRoot cannot be set here without breaking the init process.
securityContext:
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  seccompProfile:
    type: RuntimeDefault

# The resources to allocate for the pod
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 1Gi
  limits:
    memory: 4Gi

# Configuration for autoscaling
autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

# The configuration for the deployment PVC
deployment:
  pvc:
    name: data
    size: 10Gi

saves:
  pvc:
    name: saves
    size: 10Gi

backup:
  pvc:
    name: backups
    size: 10Gi

# The node selector to use for the pod
nodeSelector: {}

# The tolerations to use for the pod
tolerations: []

# The affinity to use for the pod
affinity: {}

vaultwarden.0.1.5:
replicaCount: 1

nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

image:
  repository: vaultwarden/server
  tag: 1.35.7
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

revisionHistoryLimit: 10

strategy:
  type: Recreate

serviceAccount:
  create: false
  annotations: {}
  name: default

podAnnotations: {}
podLabels: {}

podSecurityContext:
  fsGroup: 65534
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch
  seccompProfile:
    type: RuntimeDefault

securityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 65534
  runAsGroup: 65534
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: false

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 50m
    memory: 128Mi
  limits:
    memory: 512Mi

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 80
  targetPort: http
  annotations: {}

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}

persistence:
  enabled: true
  mountPath: /data
  existingClaim: vaultwarden
  create: false
  claimName: ""
  accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
  size: 10Gi
  storageClassName: longhorn-static
  volumeName: ""
  annotations: {}
  labels: {}
  keep: true

secret:
  existingSecret: vaultwarden
  create: false
  annotations: {}
  labels: {}
  adminTokenKey: admin-token
  adminToken: ""

vaultwarden:
  domain: https://key.bruno.fyi
  signupsAllowed: false
  signupsVerify: false
  requireDeviceEmail: false
  invitationsAllowed: true
  showPasswordHint: false
  webVaultEnabled: true
  sendsAllowed: true
  orgCreationUsers: all
  emergencyAccessAllowed: true
  enableDbWal: true
  admin:
    enabled: true
    disableAdminToken: false
  extraEnv: []
  extraSecretEnv: []

probes:
  liveness:
    enabled: true
    path: /
    initialDelaySeconds: 10
    periodSeconds: 10
    timeoutSeconds: 1
    failureThreshold: 3
  readiness:
    enabled: true
    path: /
    initialDelaySeconds: 10
    periodSeconds: 10
    timeoutSeconds: 1
    failureThreshold: 3

ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: ""
  annotations: {}
  hosts:
    - host: key.bruno.fyi
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: Prefix
  tls: []

virtualService:
  enabled: true
  annotations:
    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: key.bruno.fyi
    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/ttl: "300"
  gateways:
    - vaultwarden-gateway
  hosts:
    - key.bruno.fyi
  pathPrefix: /

nodeSelector: {}
affinity: {}
tolerations: []

vein.0.1.19:
image:
  repository: mbround18/vein-docker
  tag: v0.0.3
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

replicaCount: 1

serviceAccount:
  create: true
  annotations: {}
  name: ""

podSecurityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  fsGroup: 1000
  fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch
  seccompProfile:
    type: RuntimeDefault

securityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: false

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 2000m
    memory: 4Gi
  limits:
    cpu: 4000m
    memory: 18Gi

env:
  STEAM_USERNAME: "anonymous"
  STEAM_PASSWORD: ""
  APP_ID: "2131400"
  INSTALL_DIR: "/home/steam/vein"
  SERVER_NAME: "Vein Server"
  MAX_PLAYERS: "16"
  SERVER_PORT: "7777"
  QUERY_PORT: "27015"
  SERVER_PUBLIC: "true"
  BIND_ADDR: "0.0.0.0"
  HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL: "5.0"
  ADMIN_STEAM_IDS: ""
  SUPERADMIN_STEAM_IDS: ""
  STEAM_GSLT: ""
  UPDATE_ON_START: "true"
  VALIDATE: "false"
  INI_ENABLE: "true"

existingSecret:
  name: ""
  keys:
    serverPassword: SERVER_PASSWORD
    adminPassword: ADMIN_PASSWORD

service:
  type: NodePort
  bindToNode: true
  ports:
    server:
      port: 7777
      targetPort: 7777
      protocol: UDP
      nodePort: null
    query:
      port: 27015
      targetPort: 27015
      protocol: UDP
      nodePort: null
    aux:
      port: 27016
      targetPort: 27016
      protocol: UDP

wikijs.0.2.36:
global:
  secret:
    annotations: {}

replicaCount: 1
image:
  repository: ghcr.io/requarks/wiki
  tag: 2.5.277
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 100m
    memory: 256Mi
  limits:
    memory: 512Mi
service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 3000
ingress:
  enabled: false
  hosts:
    - wikijs.local
  # Kubernetes Ingress only; Istio is configured via the istio-ingress subchart below
istio-ingress:
  enabled: false
  virtualService:
    enabled: true
    hosts:
      - wikijs.local
    route:
      # The host and port targets are set dynamically in templates/00-merge-istio-routes.yaml
      host: wikijs
      port: 3000
  gateway:
    enabled: true
    selector:
      istio: ingress
    servers:
      - port:
          number: 80
          protocol: HTTP
          name: http
        hosts:
          - "*"
  tls:
    enabled: false
    mode: SIMPLE
    credentialName: ""
    httpsRedirect: false
meilisearch:
  # Dependency is available but disabled by default. Uncomment to enable and override.
  # enabled: true
  enabled: false
  image:
    repository: getmeili/meilisearch
    tag: v1.42.1
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  copyImage:
    repository: mbround18/wikijs-meilisearch-module
    tag: sha-7ae9ce1
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  secrets:
    masterKey:
      name: meilisearch-master-key
      key: MEILI_MASTER_KEY
      create: true
  resources: {}
  persistence:
    enabled: true
    accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
    size: 10Gi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 7700
  provisioning:
    enabled: true
    indexSecret: meilisearch-wikijs-data
    apiKeyDescription: Wiki.js API Key
    apiKeyActions: ["*"]
    apiKeyIndexes: ["*"]

postgres:
  enabled: true
  authMethod: scram-sha-256
  secrets:
    password:
      name: postgres-password
      key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
      create: true
    superuserPassword:
      name: postgres-superuser-password
      key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
      create: true
  ha:
    enabled: false
  image:
    repository: postgres
    tag: "18.3"
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  persistence:
    enabled: true
    accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
    size: 10Gi
  postgresql:
    db: wikijs
    user: wikijs

changedetection-io.0.1.1:
replicaCount: 1

strategy:
  type: Recreate

image:
  repository: ghcr.io/dgtlmoon/changedetection.io
  tag: 0.55.3
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 80
  targetPort: 5000
  annotations: {}

persistence:
  enabled: true
  storageClassName: ""
  size: 10Gi
  accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
  mountPath: /datastore
  annotations: {}
  # existingClaim: ""

env:
  # BASE_URL: ""
  # PLAYWRIGHT_DRIVER_URL: "ws://localhost:3000"
  # WEBDRIVER_URL: ""
  # LOGGER_LEVEL: "INFO"
  # FETCH_WORKERS: "10"
  # TZ: "UTC"
  # USE_X_SETTINGS: "1"

secrets: {}

# Sidecar browser fetcher
browser:
  sockpuppet:
    enabled: true
    image:
      repository: dgtlmoon/sockpuppetbrowser
      tag: latest
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 100m
        memory: 512Mi
      limits:
        memory: 1Gi
    securityContext:
      capabilities:
        add:
          - SYS_ADMIN
  selenium:
    enabled: false
    image:
      repository: selenium/standalone-chrome
      tag: 4
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 100m
        memory: 1Gi
      limits:
        memory: 2Gi
    env:
      SE_EVENT_BUS_HOST: localhost
      SE_EVENT_BUS_PUBLISH_PORT: 4442
      SE_EVENT_BUS_SUBSCRIBE_PORT: 4443

istio-ingress:
  enabled: false
  virtualService:
    enabled: false
    hosts:
      - changedetection.local
  # Example for production clusters:
  # externalDns:
  #   enabled: true
  #   annotations:
  #     external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: "changedetection.example.com"
  # tls:
  #   enabled: true
  #   # credentialName: "cloudflare-client-tls" # Now the default if enabled
  # gateway:
  #   servers:
  #     - port:
  #         number: 80
  #         protocol: HTTP
  #         name: http
  #       hosts: ["changedetection.example.com"]
  #     - port:
  #         number: 443
  #         protocol: HTTPS
  #         name: https
  #       hosts: ["changedetection.example.com"]

argoCd:

external-secret-resources.0.1.0:
enabled: false
annotations: {}
defaults:
  refreshPolicy: CreatedOnce
  target:
    creationPolicy: Orphan
    deletionPolicy: Retain
    annotations:
      argocd.argoproj.io/compare-options: IgnoreExtraneous
      argocd.argoproj.io/sync-options: Prune=false,Delete=false
    labels: {}
generator:
  passwordLength: 32
  allowRepeat: true
  noUpper: false
secrets: {}

backup-job.0.1.0:
# Default values for backup-job.
# Consuming charts set these under the dependency's alias, e.g.:
#
# backup-job:
#   enabled: true
#   schedule: "*/5 * * * *"
#   volumes: [...]
#   volumeMounts: [...]

# Whether the backup CronJob is rendered at all.
enabled: false

nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

backupImage: mbround18/backup-cron:v1.0.0
backupImagePullPolicy: Always

# Cron schedule the backup Job runs on.
schedule: "*/5 * * * *"

# --- Options that keep runs from stacking up ---
# Forbid: don't start a new run while the previous one is still going.
concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
# If the controller was down and missed a scheduled run by more than this
# many seconds, skip it instead of firing a backlog of catch-up runs.
startingDeadlineSeconds: 60
# Kill a hung backup Job after this long so a stuck run can't block every
# future run forever under concurrencyPolicy: Forbid.
activeDeadlineSeconds: 600
backoffLimit: 2
# How many finished Job objects Kubernetes keeps around for inspection.
successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3
# How long after finishing a Job (and its Pods) get garbage collected.
ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 300
suspend: false

# Extra environment variables for the backup container. Keys vary by which
# backup image/version a consuming chart targets (e.g. RETAIN_N_FILES vs
# FILES_TO_KEEP), so this is passed through as-is.
env: []
  # - name: INPUT_FOLDER
  #   value: /data/saves
  # - name: OUTPUT_FOLDER
  #   value: /data/backups

podSecurityContext: {}
securityContext: {}
resources: {}

# Same shape as native pod volumes/volumeMounts.
volumes: []
volumeMounts: []

nodeSelector: {}
tolerations: []
affinity: {}

podAnnotations: {}
podLabels: {}

argoCd:
  mode: auto
  instanceLabel: ""
  commonAnnotations: {}
  commonLabels: {}
